title: Add automated grid trading logic

requirements.text: Implement the automated grid order placement logic in the backend: when the bot is running, compute buy and sell grid levels based on the configured spread and order count parameters, then place limit orders on both sides of the current mid price across those grid levels.

quotes: add the automated grid trading logic

acceptanceCriteria: Grid buy and sell levels are calculated from current mid price, configured spread, and order count
Buy orders are placed below mid price and sell orders above mid price at each grid level
Placed orders are recorded and retrievable via the open orders query method
Order placement activity is logged to the activity log
If the bot is stopped, no orders are placed

requirements.text: Implement the periodic timer-driven bot loop in the backend: on each timer tick (based on the configured interval), fetch the latest market mid price, recompute the grid, cancel stale or out-of-range orders, and place new grid orders as needed.

quotes: add the automated grid trading logic

acceptanceCriteria: A recurring timer fires at the configured interval when the bot is in a running state
Each tick fetches the current mid price before recomputing the grid
Orders outside the current grid range are cancelled before new ones are placed
Each bot cycle produces activity log entries covering price fetch, cancellations, and placements
Timer stops when the bot is stopped

requirements.text: Expose a backend query method to retrieve currently open orders so the frontend OpenOrdersPanel can display the live grid state.

quotes: add the automated grid trading logic

acceptanceCriteria: A query method returns the list of open orders with price, side (buy/sell), and size
OpenOrdersPanel in the frontend successfully fetches and displays these orders
The returned data is consistent with orders placed by the grid logic

requirements.text: Update the frontend BotControlPanel to reflect the bot's running/stopped state accurately after the timer-driven loop is wired up, and surface any order placement errors reported by the backend.

quotes: add the automated grid trading logic

acceptanceCriteria: BotControlPanel shows 'Running' status when the timer loop is active
BotControlPanel shows 'Stopped' status when the bot is not running
Order placement errors returned by the backend are displayed as dismissible alerts in BotControlPanel

constraints: All backend logic must remain in the single main actor (backend/main.mo)
Do not modify immutable frontend paths: useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, or components/ui
nonGoals: Manual order entry or cancellation UI (separate feature)
Support for trading pairs other than ICP/ckUSDT
External exchange integrations beyond the existing ICDex interface
